section: **7. Cyber Security Academy — Palo Alto Systems** (Part 1)
text:
### **Cyber Security Academy — Palo Alto Systems**

#### **Objectives — Students will be able to:**

- Essentials I & II: Learn general security concepts for maintaining a secure network, the nature and scope of today's cybersecurity challenges, strategies for network defense and next-generation cybersecurity solutions.
- Cyber Security Foundation: Learn fundamentals of cybersecurity, identify concepts to recognize and potentially mitigate attacks against enterprise networks and mission critical infrastructure.
- Gateway I & II: Learn fundamental tenets of networking and concepts for maintaining a secure network computing environment.

#### **Outcomes — Students will be able to:**

- Essentials I: Formulate industry-standard designs to protect infrastructure against cybersecurity threats; apply user, application, and content ID filtering; deploy security methodologies for a secure network environment.
- Cyber Security Foundation: Apply fundamentals to identify and mitigate attacks against enterprise networks.
- Gateway I: Demonstrate knowledge of interconnected technology; identify systems needing protection; apply physical, logical, and virtual addressing through subnet mask schemes.
- Gateway II: Examine cybersecurity landscapes, attack threat vectors, exposure, vulnerabilities, and risk factors; apply knowledge to plan, design, implement, troubleshoot, and maintain network infrastructure.
- Essentials II: Assess and harden endpoints; use advanced malware research and analysis; examine mobile and cloud-based connection technologies.

### **Detailed Syllabus**

1. Fundamentals of Cyber Security (4 Hours): Security trends – Legal, Ethical and Professional Aspects – Need for Security at Multiple levels – Security Policies – Model of network security – OSI security architecture.
2. Cyber Security Concepts (4 Hours): Classical encryption (substitution, transposition, steganography) – Modern cryptography – CIA, Risks, Breaches, Threats, Attacks, Exploits – Information G

section: **7. Cyber Security Academy — Palo Alto Systems** (Part 2)
text:
ecture.
2. Cyber Security Concepts (4 Hours): Classical encryption (substitution, transposition, steganography) – Modern cryptography – CIA, Risks, Breaches, Threats, Attacks, Exploits – Information Gathering (Social Engineering, Foot Printing & Scanning).
3. Lab Session 1 (3 Hours): Tools like nmap, zenmap, Port Scanners, Network Scanners.
4. Security Algorithms (4 Hours): Asymmetric key Cryptography – Message Authentication – Digital Signatures – Applications of Cryptography – Firewalls – Types of Firewalls – User Management – VPN Security.
5. Lab Session 2 (3 Hours): Calculate message digest using SHA-1 algorithm.
6. Infrastructure Based Security (6 Hours): Network packet sniffing – Network Design Simulation – DOS/DDOS attacks – Asset Management and Audits – Vulnerabilities and Attacks – Intrusion Detection and Prevention – HIPS – Security Information Management – Network Session Analysis – System Integrity Validation.
7. Lab Session 3 (3 Hours): Implement sniffing using tools.
8. Lab Session 4 (3 Hours): Demonstrate IDS using Snort.
9. Lab Session 5 (3 Hours): Automated Attack and Penetration Tools using N-Stalker.
10. Malware (6 Hours): Explanation of Malware – Types: Virus, Worms, Trojans, Rootkits, Robots, Adware, Spywares, Ransomware, Zombies – OS Hardening – Malware Analysis.
11. Lab Session 6 (6 Hours): Building Trojans – Defeating Malware – Rootkit Hunter.

Sources: Anna University CNS Lab syllabus | AICTE emerging technology syllabus | IGNOU security certificate.

section: **8. Embedded & IoT Centre** (Part 1)
text:
### **Embedded & IoT Centre**

#### Objectives

- Learn Smart Objects and IoT Architectures.
- Learn various IoT-related protocols.
- Build simple IoT Systems using Arduino and Raspberry Pi.
- Learn data analytics and cloud in the context of IoT.
- Develop IoT infrastructure for popular applications.

#### Outcomes

- Understand the concept of IoT.
- Analyze various protocols for IoT.
- Design a PoC of an IoT system using Raspberry Pi/Arduino.
- Apply data analytics and use cloud offerings related to IoT.
- Analyze applications of IoT in real time scenarios.

### Syllabus

1. Introduction to IoT (8 Hours): Architectural Overview – Design principles – IoT Applications – Sensing – Actuation – Basics of Networking – M2M and IoT Technology Fundamentals – Devices and gateways – Data management – Business processes in IoT – Everything as a Service (XaaS) – Role of Cloud in IoT – Security aspects in IoT.
2. Elements of IoT (9 Hours): Hardware Components (Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Communication, Sensing, Actuation, I/O Interfaces) – Software Components – Programming APIs using Python/Node.js/Arduino – Communication Protocols: MQTT, ZigBee, Bluetooth, CoAP, UDP, TCP.
3. IoT Application Development (18 Hours): Solution framework for IoT applications – Implementation of Device integration – Data acquisition and integration – Device data storage (unstructured data on cloud/local server) – Authentication and authorization of devices.
4. IoT Case Studies (10 Hours): Mini projects in Industrial automation, Transportation, Agriculture, Healthcare, Home Automation.

### List of Practicals (15 Experiments)

- Familiarization with Arduino/Raspberry Pi and software installation.
- Interface LED/Buzzer with Arduino/Raspberry Pi — turn ON LED for 1 sec every 2 seconds.
- Interface Push button/Digital sensor (IR/LDR) — turn ON LED on detection.
- Interface DHT11 sensor — print temperature and humidity readings.
- Interface motor using relay — turn ON motor on button press.
- Interface OLED —

section: **8. Embedded & IoT Centre** (Part 2)
text:
igital sensor (IR/LDR) — turn ON LED on detection.
- Interface DHT11 sensor — print temperature and humidity readings.
- Interface motor using relay — turn ON motor on button press.
- Interface OLED — print temperature and humidity.
- Interface Bluetooth — send sensor data to smartphone.
- Interface Bluetooth — turn LED ON/OFF via smartphone.
- Upload temperature and humidity data to ThingSpeak cloud.
- Retrieve temperature and humidity data from ThingSpeak cloud.
- Install MySQL database on Raspberry Pi and perform basic SQL queries.
- Publish temperature data to MQTT broker.
- Subscribe to MQTT broker for temperature data.
- Create TCP server on Arduino/Raspberry Pi — respond with humidity data.
- Create UDP server — respond with humidity data to UDP client.

section: --- 9. e-Yantra IIT Bombay Remote Centre & Code Tantra Coding Learning Centre
text:
### e-Yantra — IIT Bombay Remote Centre

e-Yantra is an IIT Bombay initiative. MSAJCE is a Remote Centre for e-Yantra under IIT Bombay.

#### **Objectives — Students will be able to:**

- Learn the function of basic components of robots.
- Study various types of end effectors and sensors.
- Impart knowledge in Robot kinematics and Programming.
- Learn Robot safety issues and economics.

#### **Outcomes — Students will be able to:**

- Understand embedded systems and robotics technology.
- Design, develop, program and test robots for various applications.
- Participate in national and international robotics competitions confidently.
- Use robots to solve real life problems.

### Code Tantra — Coding Learning Centre

Industry Partner: Code Tantra

#### **Objectives — Students will be able to:**

- Understand the syntax and semantics of various programming languages (C, C++, Java, Python).
- Get a new perspective of problem solving by choosing appropriate programming domains for software product development.
- Make students competent data analyzers or data scientists by learning Big Data and Hadoop.
- Increase the ability of program testing and debugging using Selenium testing tool.
- Prepare students for competitive based learning by participating in global level coding contests.

#### **Outcomes — Students will be able to:**

- Design and implement software applications using C, C++ basic constructs.
- Develop software products for industries in various programming platforms.
- Understand the key issues in big data management and its associated applications in intelligent business and scientific computing.
- Test and debug various software products effectively.
- Develop real-time web and mobile applications.

section: --- 10. Gaming & AR/VR Centre (Part 1)
text:
### Gaming & AR/VR Centre

#### **Objectives — Students will be able to:**

- Learn technical and design aspects of AR and VR.
- Create 3D design, Visualization and Animation for various objects and scenes.
- Apply virtual and augmented reality methods, tools and equipment in industry, education and social life.
- Provide an overview of the concepts and working of AR-VR techniques.

#### **Outcomes — Students will be able to:**

- Build basic applications considering technical and design aspects of AR and VR.
- Develop PC/Mobile-based VR applications.
- Develop 3D Visualization and Visualization projects.
- Design applications in Gaming, Architecture, Manufacturing, Retail, Healthcare, etc.

### Syllabus Modules

1. Introduction to Virtual Reality (6 Hours): Fundamental Concept and Components of VR – Primary Features and Present Development – Computer graphics – Real time computer graphics – Flight Simulation – Benefits of VR – Historical development – 3D Computer Graphics: Virtual world space, positioning the virtual observer, perspective projection, human vision, stereo perspective projection, 3D clipping, Colour theory, Simple 3D modelling, Illumination models, Reflection models, Shading algorithms, Radiosity, Hidden Surface Removal, Realism-Stereographic image.
2. Interactive Techniques in VR (6 Hours): From 2D to 3D – 3D space curves – 3D boundary representation – Geometric Transformations – Frames of reference – Modeling transformations – Instances – Picking – Flying – Scaling the VE – Collision detection – Generic VR system: Virtual environment, Computer environment, VR technology, Model of interaction, VR Systems.
3. Visual Computation in VR (6 Hours): Animating the Virtual Environment – Dynamics of numbers – Linear and Nonlinear interpolation – Animation of objects – Shape and object inbetweening – Free-form deformation – Particle system – Physical Simulation: Objects in gravitational field, Rotating wheels, Elastic collisions, Projectiles, Simple pendulum,

section: --- 10. Gaming & AR/VR Centre (Part 2)
text:
ects – Shape and object inbetweening – Free-form deformation – Particle system – Physical Simulation: Objects in gravitational field, Rotating wheels, Elastic collisions, Projectiles, Simple pendulum, Springs, Flight dynamics.
4. Augmented and Mixed Reality (6 Hours): Taxonomy, technology and features of AR – Difference between AR and VR – Challenges with AR – AR systems and functionality – AR methods – Visualization techniques – Wireless displays in educational AR – Mobile projection interfaces – Marker-less tracking – Enhancing interactivity in AR – Evaluating AR systems.
5. Multiple Models of Input and Output Interface in VR (6 Hours): Human factors – VR Hardware: Head-coupled displays, Acoustic hardware, Integrated VR systems – VR Software: Modeling virtual world, Physical simulation, VR toolkits, Introduction to VRML – Input: Tracker, Sensor, Digital Glove, Movement Capture, Video-based Input, 3D Menus, 3D Scanner – Output: Visual/Auditory/Haptic Devices.
6. Application of VR in Digital Entertainment (6 Hours): VR in Film & TV Production – VR in Physical Exercises and Games – Demonstration of Digital Entertainment by VR.

### Practicals (9 Hours)

- Architecture design using VR
- CRO experiments using VR
- Qualitative analysis in Chemistry using VR
- Engine assembly/disassembly using VR
- Human anatomy exploration
- Simulation of blood circulation
- Simulation of Flight/Vehicle/Space Station
- Building Electronic circuits using VR
- Developing Virtual Classroom with multiplayer

Resources from: AICTE | IIT Varanasi, India

section: 11. Bentley's BIM Centre and Centre for CAE — Altair & CATIA
text:
### Bentley's Building Information Modelling (BIM) Centre

#### Objectives

- Gain basic theoretical knowledge and practical skills of BIM techniques.
- Interpret 2D & 3D drawings and structural analysis.
- Understand regulations as per National Building Code.
- Prepare students for actual work situations through practical training.

#### Outcomes

- Prepare a component to meet desired needs within realistic constraints (economic, environmental, social, ethical, sustainability).
- Create 2D drawings of building, industrial structures and various framed structures using AUTOCAD software.
- Create and simulate a virtual building model using REVIT ARCHITECTURE software with intelligent building elements.
- Design and analyse different types of structures (liquid retaining structures, bridge components, retaining wall, industrial and commercial structures) in 3D form using STAAD.Pro software to predict performances before construction.

Software Used: AUTOCAD, REVIT ARCHITECTURE, STAAD.Pro

### Centre for CAE — Supported by Altair & CATIA

Industry Partner: Altair

#### Objectives

- Understand and interpret drawings of machine components.
- Prepare assembly drawings using standard CAD packages.
- Gain practical experience in handling 2D drafting and 3D modeling software systems.
- Comprehend, design, analyze, and suggest novel materials, products and solutions for contemporary manufacturing issues.

#### Outcomes

- Develop CAD models for Hypermesh.
- Import and export CAD data and generate .hm files.
- Attain accurate and high quality meshes in 1D, 2D and 3D elements.
- Carry out Linear Static Analysis.
- Carry out Dynamic Analysis.

Software Used: Altair HyperMesh, CATIA

section: 12. Communication Language Learning Centre
text:
### Communication Language Learning Centre

#### Objective

- Acquire knowledge in foreign languages and become proficient in communication skills — Listening, Speaking, Reading and Writing (LSRW) — to achieve employability standard at international level.

#### Outcomes — Students will be able to:

- Become expert bilinguals to learn work ethic and business etiquette in career opportunities.
- Gain deeper knowledge and higher understanding of foreign languages.
- Boost professional value in Business, Social Science, Humanities, Technology and Science.
- Get exposure to culture ingrained in the people of various countries.
- Improve higher thinking skills, new learning strategies, and exposure to advanced technologies.
- Grow as leaders in many leading sectors to drive for innovation.

Focus: Language Skills (LSRW — Listening, Speaking, Reading, Writing), Foreign Language Proficiency, International Employability, Business Communication.

section: 13. RAISE Centre of Excellence — MIT Square London (Part 1)
text:
### **RAISE Centre of Excellence (CoE) — Powered by MIT Square, London**

Established: June 2022

### **About MIT Square Services Private Limited**

MIT Square is an ISO Certified Company headquartered in Bangalore (Silicon Valley of India) and registered office in London (Silicon Valley of UK). It is a registered MSME recognised by the Ministry of MSME, Govt. of India and recognised by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Govt. of UK. Network offices in USA, Australia and Singapore. MIT stands for 'Management and Innovation for Transformation'. Square represents 'People + Process + Product + Profit' — tagline: 'We transform your life'. Core technical team from IITs and Universities in London.

MIT Square is a globally trusted brand in EduTech, HealthTech, AgroTech and GreenTech supporting: incubation/start-up, business operations, talent enablement (training & learning), talent acquisition, innovations, patent filing, product design & development, manufacturing and supply of technological and non-technological products.

### **About RAISE**

RAISE = R – Research | A – Arts & Applications | I – Innovation & Intelligence | S – Science & Technology | E – Entrepreneurship

The CoE for IoT at MSAJCE campus was established in June 2022 through MoU with MIT Square London.

Vision: Enable the institution as an innovation hub in emerging technologies.

### **Key Initiatives — i-10 Model**

- Innovation & Research
- Ideas to Prototypes & Products
- Inductive Programs
- Internships & Talent Transformation
- International Publications
- I-Centric Events
- IP Rights & Protection
- Intrapreneurship & Entrepreneurship
- Institution & Industry Connects
- Incubation, Investments & Fundings

#### **Specific Initiatives**

Abroad Studies | Abroad Traineeships | Abroad Internships | Abroad Placements | Abroad Mentorships | Abroad Fellowships | Abroad Inventorships | Startup Founderships | Disruptive Technologies | Product Development | CSR Activities

### **Detailed Objectives**

- Crea

section: 13. RAISE Centre of Excellence — MIT Square London (Part 2)
text:
Placements | Abroad Mentorships | Abroad Fellowships | Abroad Inventorships | Startup Founderships | Disruptive Technologies | Product Development | CSR Activities

### **Detailed Objectives**

- Create innovative applications and domain capability for Smart City, Smart Health, Smart Manufacturing, Smart Agriculture.
- Build industry capable talent, startup community and entrepreneurial ecosystem.
- Provide ecosystem for innovation and entrepreneurship.
- Energize research mind-set and reduce costs in R&D; with neutral, interoperable, multi-technology stack laboratory facilities.
- Reduce import dependency on technology components and promote indigenization.
- Position the institution as a provider of end-to-end solutions in education and technology.
- Provide training and learning (including internships) both online and offline.
- Provide environment for product creation, testing, validation and incubation.
- Provide learning in breakthrough technologies: AR/VR, Data Analytics, AI.

section: --- 14. RAISE Centre — Benefits, Nodal Centre Details and Contact Information (Part 1)
text: ### RAISE Centre — Benefits for Students and Nodal Centre Details #### Benefits of RAISE CoE - A great learning and leadership opportunity. - Become a coding guru. - Spread Techno-Entrepreneurship Culture within the Campus through events, workshops and hackathons. - Internship Opportunities. - Opportunity to work in startups or prestigious companies. - Opportunity to study abroad. - Opportunity to intern or work full-time abroad. - Personalised LinkedIn recommendation. - Letter of Recommendation after successful Internship/techno programs completion. ### MSAJCE as RAISE Nodal Centre — Benefits - A fully Techno-Entrepreneurship Innovation lab with hardware and software tools with licenses for development and research projects. - Recognition as one of the top National Academies for the innovation lab. - Essential curriculum and resource kits for comprehensive training and industry readiness. - Scalable infrastructure to extend training to corporates and professionals from other industries. - Up to 10 years assured training support from MIT Square to empower faculty, research scholars and students. - Based on NEP 2020, support provided in regional languages as well. #### Government Initiatives Supported Digital India | Make in India | Startup India | Standup India | Skill India | Self-reliant India (Atmanirbhar Bharat) #### --- MSAJCE Contact Information | | | |------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Institution | Mohamed Sathak A.J. **Transport Committee:** A committee headed by a Convener manages smooth bus operations. One teaching and one supporting staff are in-charge of each bus. The committee meets once every six months; the Principal attends the meeting.

section: 1. CSI — Computer Society of India (Part 1)
text:
### Vision: "IT for Masses"

Keeping in mind the interest of IT professionals and computer users, CSI works towards making the profession an area of choice amongst all sections of society. CSI regularly organizes conferences, conventions, lectures, projects, and awards. It also provides training for physically challenged citizens ('Punarjani') and assists governments in formulating IT strategy and planning.

### Region VII Covers:

Tamil Nadu, Pondicherry, Andaman and Nicobar, Kerala, Lakshadweep.

### Kanchipuram Chapter

The Society has developed a well-established network across the country. Activities for students include lecture meetings, seminars, conferences, training programs, programming contests, and industrial visits. CSI's Educational Directorate undertakes certification of professionals on the latest technologies and offers distance education in Business Domain areas.

### Awards Received:

section: 2. IEEE — Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
text:
The IEEE is a United States-based non-profit organization and the world's leading professional association for the advancement of technology. It covers areas ranging from aerospace systems, electrical, electronics, computers, telecommunications, biomedical engineering, electric power, and consumer electronics. IEEE publishes technical literature and highly qualified research articles across all engineering domains.

### Student Branch Activities:

- Participation in regional conferences, workshops and competitions
- Development of leadership, interpersonal and team building skills
- Scholarships and project/design programs and student paper contests
- Establishment of a Student Branch Library with IEEE publications
- Access to IEEE online services and resources; establish a website
- Organize various awareness programs for school students and public

section: 3. IETE — Institution of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineers (Part 1)
text:
IETE is one of the leading Professional Societies in India for Electronics and Communication Engineers. MSAJCE established its IETE Students Forum on 16/08/2017 with 100 active members. The forum organizes guest lectures, seminars, workshops, and conferences. Student members benefit from technical visits, practical training, and project work in R&D; laboratories and industries.

### ***IETE Publications:***

- IETE Journal of Education
- IETE Journal of Research
- IETE Technical Review

### ***Branch Counselors:***

- Branch Counselor 1: Dr. I. Manju
- Branch Counselor 2: Mrs. S. Anusuya

### ***Goals of IETE:***

- Improving the standard of education
- Train students in emerging areas to meet new opportunities
- Encouraging outside-the-classroom studies, practical work and seminars
- Better employment of students on completion of their courses
- Substantial increase of IETE membership and nationwide distribution

### ***Benefits of ISF (IETE Students Forum):***

- Priority for publishing papers in Journal of Education
- Can subscribe to IETE Journal of Research and IETE Technical Review (available online)
- ISF students can attend IETE technical programs in India at reduced registration fees
- ISF members are issued with an Identity Card

SAE is a US-based, globally active professional association and standards developing organization for engineering professionals. section: 5. ISHRAE — Indian Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air Conditioning Engineers
text:
ISHRAE was founded in 1981 at New Delhi by a group of eminent HVAC&R; professionals. It operates from 41 chapters and sub-chapters spread all over India, with HQ in Delhi. ISHRAE works with various Govt. Ministries/Departments including BIS, BEE (ECBC), MoEFCC (Ozone Cell), and NCCD (Ministry of Agriculture). It is looked upon as a repository of technical knowledge in the HVAC&R; and Building Industry field by peer organizations and the Government of India.

### Focus Areas:

- Sustainability
- Green Buildings
- Energy Efficiency
- Environmental Responsibility
- Indoor Air Quality
- Fire & Safety

### Student Branch Activities:

- Excellent networking opportunities to interact with professionals, industry leaders, and key decision makers
- Forum for professional development and continuous training on fundamentals and latest technologies
- Easy access to all ISHRAE Technical Publications, Software, Workshops, Conferences and Training Programs
- Opportunity to work in Chapter Working Committee and Headquarter activities
- Platform for effective marketing and launching of new products through exhibitions and advertisements
- International exposure through International Associates

section: Girls Hostel Outing and Security Rules
text: Girls Hostel Outing Rules: Hostellers are generally not permitted to leave the campus except on holidays and require prior written permission from their Head of Department (HOD) and the hostel warden. For outstation travel or going home, written permission from the warden or superintendent is mandatory. Parents must provide a list of authorized visitors or local guardians; outsiders and friends are strictly prohibited from entering rooms. Return times are strictly enforced. Location: Sholinganallur (5 km from campus).

text: MSAJCE Merit Scholarships: Students with a cut-off of 180+ out of 200 are eligible for merit-based tuition waivers. Female students receive a special 10% discount on tuition fees. section: Anti-Ragging and Lateral Entry
text: Anti-Ragging: Headed by the Principal with dedicated squads. Zero tolerance policy—punishments include FIR filing, hostel expulsion, and admission cancellation. Lateral Entry: Admissions conducted via TN-LEA counselling. Candidates must hold a Diploma in Engineering/Technology or a B.Sc. with Mathematics. IMPORTANT: Lateral Entry is strictly for DIRECT SECOND YEAR only. There is NO lateral entry option for the third year.

section: MSAJCE — An Overview and Establishment
text:
MSAJCE — An Overview

The Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering (MSAJCE), established on 5th July 2001 under the aegis of Mohamed Sathak Trust, is approved by AICTE New Delhi, affiliated to Anna University Chennai and Government of Tamil Nadu. MSAJCE strives to continuously upgrade its facilities to provide quality technical education to meet the industrial and societal needs, by providing skill-based training with state of the art facilities.

The college campus, sprawling over 70 acres of greenery, is located inside the SIPCOT IT Park, Siruseri, and is surrounded by multinational IT companies such as TCS, CTS, Intellect, Aspire, Steria, Polaris, FSS, etc. MSAJCE has good infrastructure facilities such as spacious classrooms, drawing halls, laboratories, seminar halls, computer facilities with internet and wifi connectivity, library, workshops, auditorium and playground. The college is well connected by Road, Rail and Air from various parts of the country. A team of well qualified and experienced faculty supports the teaching and learning process. The college provides equal importance for academic, co-curricular and extracurricular activities. MSAJCE provides all kinds of training for the professional and overall transformation of the students.

section: Mohamed Sathak Trust — History, Founding and Management
text:
Mohamed Sathak Trust — Management

Mohamed Sathak Trust, a charitable and educational organization, was established on 26 October 1973 at Kilakarai, by the Philanthropic Mohamed Sathak Family of Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram District, Tamil Nadu, with the sole purpose of helping deprived people from rural and financially challenged backgrounds to obtain quality education.

The first self-financing engineering college in the country was established by the Trust in 1984 at Kilakarai. From that day onwards, the Trust has been contributing to provide quality education with academic excellence to meet the requirements of industries at national and international levels.

The Trust has an impeccable track record of running quality educational institutions from higher secondary level to undergraduate and postgraduate levels in various disciplines such as Engineering Technology, Arts and Science, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, Nursing and Medical Sciences. It has 18 educational institutions at present in Kilakarai, Ramanathapuram and Chennai.

The Trust's guiding philosophy: "God will not change the condition of a people until they try to change themselves." The Trust focuses on doing right things without any bias and with a charitable mind to build institutions of academic excellence and help in building the nation.

section: Chairman Message and Principal Message
text:
Chairman Message

Dear Parents and Prospective Students,

Welcome to Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering, Chennai. Choosing the right institute to pursue your higher studies is one of the most important decisions that you will ever make. It can be the key for the door of your lifelong opportunity for sustainable growth and service. We want you to base your choice at the right place with relevant and reliable education.

The success depends on the effort you invest in your own intellectual and professional development. Invest wisely and be assured that MSAJCE faculty and staff pledge their best efforts in helping you to achieve your educational goals. Our standards are challenging and we are dedicating ourselves in helping you to meet the standards. We will judge our institution's success by how best you succeed in realizing your potential as a student and later in your professional career. I invite you to visit our magnificent campus, to witness our state-of-the-art facilities including the Technology Centres, interact with students and faculty, and convince yourselves on why so many bright students have made MSAJCE their choice of institution.

I am confident that you will take pride in joining our college.

---

Principal Message — Dr. K.S. Srinivasan Ph.D, Principal, MSAJCE

Dear Parents and Prospective Students,

As Principal, I am impressed by the commitment of our management and the faculty for the provision of an excellent all-round education with state of the art facilities to our students so that they become multidimensional rather than uni-dimensional. Acquiring information is no longer the main focus of education; instead the main aim is to build the powers of human mind and spirit. MSAJCE believes in providing our students an environment which is rich in knowledge and supportive for their extra-curricular interests. We evaluate our students on the basis of their physical, mental, social, emotional and intellectual development. MSAJCE is dedicated to developing skilled and outstanding students who can pursue their interest and strength to the best of their ability. Our enriched students will be able to actively face Industry 4.0 challenges as well as build a strong society for a better world. We practice a holistic approach with an enabled new structure that stimulates young minds in terms of innovative and creative thinking with great freedom to learn with different perceptiveness.

I welcome you all and wish a Memorable Studentship. I hope you will be able to achieve greater heights and bring laurels to our Institute.

ELIGIBILITY FOR STUDENTS FROM OTHER STATES
(Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Kerala and all Northern States)

Selection of candidates will be based on the qualifying marks (Q) obtained in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry at the Plus Two or Intermediate level. The qualifying marks (Q) are calculated as follows:

Q = (Mathematics / 2) + ((Physics + Chemistry) / 4)

where M, P, and C represent the percentage marks in Mathematics, Physics and Chemistry, respectively.

section: Department Overview — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)
text:
Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science — MSAJCE

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the process of creating machines to think like humans. AI is a term used to describe the simulation of human intelligence processes in machines. It typically involves sophisticated algorithms that enable computers to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, such as visual perception, speech recognition and decision making.

Data Science is a field of knowledge which applies advanced statistical and mathematical techniques to large datasets in order to extract knowledge from them. Data can be structured like a spreadsheet or unstructured like text or video data. Data science is the application of techniques from statistics, mathematics, computer programming and other fields to solve real-world problems using information technology.

Due to the exploration of the Internet, AI and Data Science deliver data-driven solutions using computational principles, methods, and systems for extracting information from data and modern computational systems that reveal capabilities of insight, way of thinking, learning, and action that are typical of human intelligence.

B.Tech Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS) has emerged as one of the most high-growth, dynamic, and lucrative careers in technology since 2015. This course aims at providing core technologies such as artificial intelligence, data mining, and data modeling, and also gives intensive inputs in areas of machine learning and big data analytics. Students gain cross-disciplinary skills across fields such as statistics, computer science, machine learning, and logic. Career opportunities exist in healthcare, business, e-commerce, social networking companies, climatology, biotechnology, genetics, and other important areas. The major focus of this programme is to equip students with statistical, mathematical reasoning, machine learning, knowledge discovery, and visualization skills.

The programme also deals with core principles and ethical issues in Data Science and AI, and motivates students to explore cutting-edge applications related to Big Data, Neural Networks, and Deep Learning. Applications like Alexa/Siri, Erica/Sophia, Tesla's driverless car, Chat-bot, etc. are becoming common. AI-powered Robots can take over human life-endangering jobs like firefighting, deep-sea oil drilling, and mining works with high mortality rates.

Career Opportunities for AIDS Graduates (across Manufacturing, Software, Research, Education, Defense, Banking & Finance, e-Commerce, Transport and Health Science):
• Software Engineer
• Software Tester
• Application Developer
• Information and Multimedia Designer
• Medical Information Scientist
• Technology Developer
• IT Specialist
• Technical Consultant
• Quality Analyst
• Information Network Manager
• Data Quality Analyst

section: B.Tech AIDS Programme Details, Vision, Mission and OBE (PEOs, PSOs, POs)
text:
B.Tech — Artificial Intelligence and Data Science (AIDS)

Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry)
No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry)
Intake / No. of Seats: Total - 30 (Government - 15, Management - 15)
Eligibility: 10+2 system of Education. Must have secured a pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in the qualifying examination.
Curriculum & Syllabus: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf
Career Opportunities: Public Sector Undertaking | Government Sector
Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

Vision: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Data Science using through sustainable practices.

Mission:
M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology.
M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society.
M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability.

Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs):
PEO1: Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability.
PEO2: Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements.
PEO3: Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms.

Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs):
PSO1: Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies / tools for real time applications of Industry.
PSO2: Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications.

Programme Outcomes (POs):
PO1. Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.
PO2. Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles.
PO3. Design/Development Of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health, safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.
PO4. Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions.
PO5. Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities.
PO6. The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and consequent responsibilities relevant to professional engineering practice.
PO7. Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development.
PO8. Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice.
PO9. Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings.
PO10. Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large.
PO11. Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work.
PO12. Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

section: AI and Data Science Growth in India 2025–2030 — Key Drivers and Job Roles
text:
AI and Data Science Growth in India by 2025–2030

The Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Data Science sectors in India are expected to have explosive growth over the next five years. AI-driven solutions and data analytics are driving better decisions across all sectors, and the demand for professionals in these fields is expected to grow significantly.

Key Drivers:
1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — Healthcare, Finance, E-commerce, Telecommunications, Automotive, and Manufacturing. AI will be used for Predictive analytics, Personalized services, Data-driven decision-making, and Automation. AI-driven solutions in core business functions (HR, finance, customer service).
2. Government Support and Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, and the National AI Mission initiatives boost AI research, startups, and adoption across public services. India aims to be a global leader in AI for Smart cities, Healthcare, and Agriculture.
3. Rising Demand for Data-Driven Decision Making — Customer segmentation, Fraud detection, and Supply chain optimization.
4. Growth in AI-Powered Startups — AI startups expected to double in the next few years, creating job opportunities for AI engineers, data scientists, and product managers.
5. Integration of AI into Business Functions — Finance, retail, e-commerce, and manufacturing increasingly adopting AI-based automation to streamline operations, reduce costs, and offer personalized experiences.

Key AI and Data Science Job Roles in India (2025–2030):

1. Data Scientist
Roles: Analyze large datasets to derive actionable insights, build predictive models, help organizations make data-driven decisions.
Skills: Python, R, SQL, Machine Learning Algorithms, Statistical analysis, Data visualization (Tableau, Power BI), Deep learning.
Demand: High demand across healthcare, finance, retail, and e-commerce.

2. Machine Learning Engineer
Roles: Build and deploy machine learning models, implement algorithms, optimize model performance for real-world applications.
Skills: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, Neural networks, Cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), Algorithm optimization.
Demand: Rising, especially in startups and tech giants as companies adopt more AI solutions.

3. AI Research Scientist
Roles: Conduct cutting-edge research in AI; focus on Deep learning, Reinforcement learning, Computer vision, and NLP.
Skills: Machine learning algorithms, Python, Mathematical modeling, Research publications, Deep learning frameworks.
Demand: High demand due to growing AI investment from global tech companies and research labs.

4. Data Analyst
Roles: Collect, process, and analyze data to uncover trends, create dashboards, and support decision-making with data-driven insights.
Skills: Excel, SQL, Python, Tableau, Power BI, R, SAS.
Demand: Increasing in finance, retail, and healthcare as companies focus on data-driven decision-making.

5. Data Engineer
Roles: Design, construct, and maintain large-scale data pipelines; work with big data technologies to ensure data is stored, processed, and accessible.
Skills: SQL, Python, Hadoop, Spark, Kafka, AWS, Google Cloud, ETL processes.
Demand: Rising with the growth of big data and cloud computing in data-driven companies.

6. AI Product Manager
Roles: Oversee the development and deployment of AI-powered products; manage cross-functional teams of engineers and data scientists.
Skills: Product lifecycle management, Agile methodology, Machine learning, Project management, Business strategy.
Demand: Rising with AI product development across industries.

7. Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
Roles: Design and develop BI tools to collect, store, and analyze business data; help businesses make strategic decisions using AI-driven insights.
Skills: SQL, Power BI, Tableau, data visualization, reporting tools, Python.
Demand: Increasing as companies shift to data-driven strategies, particularly in e-commerce and retail.

8. Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineer
Roles: Work on algorithms and models that allow machines to process and understand human language, like chatbots, virtual assistants, and sentiment analysis.
Skills: Python, NLP frameworks (NLTK, SpaCy, Hugging Face), Machine learning, Deep learning, Text mining.
Demand: Growing in e-commerce, finance, and telecommunications with the rise of AI chatbots, voice assistants, and text analytics.

section: Industry-Specific AI Job Trends, Expected Salary Growth and Required Skills & Certifications
text:
Industry-Specific AI and Data Science Job Trends:

1. Healthcare Sector — Diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Bioinformatics, Healthcare data analysis, and Medical AI applications.
2. Finance and Fintech — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Automated trading, and Personalized financial services. Employers: Banks, Insurance companies, and Fintech startups.
3. E-Commerce and Retail — Personalization, Dynamic pricing, Inventory optimization, and Recommendation systems.
4. Automotive and Autonomous Vehicles — Autonomous driving, Vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, Predictive maintenance, Computer vision, Sensor data processing, and AI-powered automotive solutions.
5. Telecommunication Sector — Network optimization, Customer service Chatbots, Predictive maintenance, 5G, IoT, and Edge computing.

Expected Growth in AI and Data Science Jobs:

Job Market Growth: Expected CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 30–40%. By 2030, India could see hundreds of thousands of AI and data science job openings in Tech companies, Startups, and Industries adopting data-driven transformation.

Salary Trends:
- Entry-level roles (0–2 years experience): Rs. 6 – Rs. 12 Lakhs per annum
- Mid-level roles (3–5 years experience): Rs. 12 – Rs. 25 Lakhs per annum
- Senior-level roles (5+ years experience): Rs. 25 – Rs. 50 Lakhs per annum
- AI Research Scientists: Rs. 30 – Rs. 70 Lakhs per annum

Skills and Certifications Required for AI and Data Science Jobs:

Technical Skills:
1. Python, R, and SQL for data manipulation and machine learning algorithms.
2. Machine learning frameworks: TensorFlow, Keras, and PyTorch.
3. Cloud platforms (AWS, Google Cloud, Azure) for deploying AI models.
4. Big data tools such as Hadoop, Spark, and Kafka.
5. Advanced certifications from platforms like Coursera, edX, and Udacity.

Recommended Certifications:
1. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer
2. AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty
3. Data Science Specialization by IBM
4. Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng

page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Department Overview & Programme Details — B.E. AI & ML
text: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Overview: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a technology that makes machines reproduce human behavior. Machine Learning (ML) is a sub-part of AI by which machines automatically learn from past data without programming. AI and ML together innovate human-like intelligent computer systems to solve complex problems. B.Tech in CSE with Specialization in AIML (CSE-AIML) provides budding engineers with courses dedicated to frontiers in AI and ML with a foundation of Computer Science & Engineering. This course enhances knowledge of computer technologies including programming, coding, database and web development. Engineers with AI expertise will be needed in crucial domains such as Healthcare, Industry 4.0, Finance, Agriculture, Security, Law, and Environment Management. The curriculum covers Computational Mathematics, core Computer Science, and latest advancements in AI and ML. Specialization areas include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, computer vision, data mining, Explainable AI, Generative Adversarial Networks, Multimodal AI and Regenerative AI. Tools and technologies: Python, R, TensorFlow, Spark, Hadoop. Career Prospects: Big Data Engineer, Business Intelligence Developer, Data Scientist, Machine Learning Engineer, Research Scientist, AI Data Analyst, AI Engineer, Robotics Scientist. Recruiting companies include Microsoft, Amazon, Goldman Sachs, Oracle GBU, Cisco, Dell Technologies, Accenture. Programme Details — B.E. Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning: Duration: 4 years (Regular) / 3 years (Lateral Entry). No. of Semesters: 8 (Regular) / 6 (Lateral Entry). Total Seats: 60. Government Quota: 30. Management Quota: 30. Eligibility: 10+2 with pass in Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Curriculum: 2021 Regulation — https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf. Scope for Higher Studies: M.E. / M.Tech. / M.B.A.

page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Vision, Mission, PEOs, PSOs & Programme Outcomes (POs)
text: Vision of AIML Department: To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Engineers using through sustainable practices. Mission of AIML Department: M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Artificial Intelligence and Data Science through innovative teaching and learning methodology. M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society. M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability. Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs): PEO1. Graduates will be prepared for analysing, designing, developing and testing the software solutions and products with creativity and sustainability. PEO2. Graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3. Graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms. Program Specific Outcomes (PSOs): PSO1. Develop creative solutions by adapting emerging technologies/tools for real time applications of Industry. PSO2. Apply the acquired knowledge to develop software solutions and innovative mobile apps for various automation applications. Programme Outcomes (POs): PO1 Engineering Knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions. PO3 Design/Development of Solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet specified needs. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data. PO5 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools. PO6 The Engineer and Society: Apply reasoning to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues. PO7 Environment and Sustainability: Understand the impact of engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts. PO8 Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and norms of engineering practice. PO9 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, member or leader in diverse teams and multidisciplinary settings. PO10 Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities, write reports, design documentation, make presentations. PO11 Project Management and Finance: Demonstrate knowledge of engineering and management principles as a member and leader. PO12 Life-long Learning: Recognize the need for, and engage in independent and life-long learning in technological change.

page_title: Department of Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: AI & ML Job Roles, Salary Trends & Employment Forecast India 2025-2030
text: AI and ML Employment Forecast in India 2025-2030: The AI and ML sectors in India are poised for substantial growth over the next five years. Key Drivers: 1. Increased AI Adoption Across Industries — AI/ML adopted across healthcare, finance, e-commerce, retail, manufacturing, agriculture. Companies leveraging AI/ML for automation, data analytics, predictive analytics, personalization, customer service. Indian companies like Infosys, Wipro, TCS investing heavily in AI/ML. 2. Government Initiatives — Digital India, National AI Strategy, AI for All, AI-enabled Smart Cities, AI-powered education. 3. AI and Automation in Enterprises — Robotic Process Automation (RPA) integrated with AI and NLP creating job opportunities. 4. R&D and Innovation — Google, Microsoft, IBM, Intel have set up R&D centers in India. 5. Increased Investment in Startups — AI startups attracting substantial venture capital investment. 6. Healthcare, Autonomous Vehicles, Smart Cities — Major AI integration areas. Key AI and ML Job Roles (2025-2030): Machine Learning Engineer — Develop and deploy ML models. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, neural networks, deep learning. High demand in e-commerce, finance, automotive, manufacturing. Data Scientist — Analyze and interpret complex data. Skills: Python, R, SQL, Matplotlib, Seaborn, ML algorithms, big data tools. In demand in finance, retail, healthcare, telecom. AI Research Scientist — Conduct cutting-edge research in NLP, computer vision, reinforcement learning. Skills: Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, mathematical modeling. Deep Learning Engineer — Build neural networks for computer vision, NLP, speech recognition. Skills: TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch. NLP Engineer — Develop AI systems for human language using ML techniques. Skills: SpaCy, NLTK, sentiment analysis, speech recognition. AI Software Engineer — Design and build AI applications. Skills: Python, C++, Java, TensorFlow, cloud computing, Docker, Kubernetes. AI Ethics Specialist — Ensure ethical AI, bias reduction, fairness, transparency. AI/ML Product Manager — Manage AI product development. Skills: Agile, data analysis, market research. Expected Salary Ranges: Entry-level (0-2 years): Rs. 6-12 Lakhs per annum. Mid-level (3-5 years): Rs. 12-20 Lakhs per annum. Senior-level (5+ years): Rs. 20-35 Lakhs per annum. AI Research Scientist: Rs. 25-50 Lakhs per annum. AI/ML Project Managers (Senior): Rs. 30-60 Lakhs per annum. Job Market Growth: CAGR of 30-40% in next 5 years. Total AI jobs could double or triple.

section: Industry-Specific AI Trends, Certifications & Targeted Job Applications
text: Industry-Specific AI and ML Job Trends: Healthcare — Medical diagnostics, Personalized treatment, Drug discovery, Robot-assisted surgery, Medical AI, Bioinformatics, and healthcare data analytics. Finance and Banking — Fraud detection, Credit scoring, Personalized banking services, Automated trading, Financial data scientists, Quantitative analysts, AI-enabled risk management. ML applications in Fintech will create opportunities for specialists in Blockchain, smart contracts, and AI in Cryptocurrency. Automotive (Autonomous Vehicles) — Self-driving cars and Smart transportation systems. AI and ML professionals required for computer vision, sensor fusion, and real-time decision-making for autonomous vehicles. Retail and E-commerce — AI transforming customer experience through personalized recommendations, dynamic pricing. Demand for professionals in e-commerce AI, recommendation algorithms, and supply chain optimization. Certifications and Skills to Upskill: 1. Python and R language for data manipulation and model development. 2. TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras for deep learning. 3. Data science tools: Pandas, NumPy, SciPy for data wrangling and analysis. 4. NLP frameworks: SpaCy, Hugging Face for text processing and sentiment analysis. 5. Google AI Certification and Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng (Coursera). 6. Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer. 7. AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. 8. Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate. 9. IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate. 10. Data Science Professional Certificate. 11. Certified Data Scientist (CDS). 12. Deep Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng. 13. Advanced Machine Learning Specialization. 14. Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning. Targeted Job Applications: AI-centric companies — Google, Microsoft, Amazon, NVIDIA, IBM, TCS, and Infosys. Startups — India's growing AI/ML startups in Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune offer hands-on work in cutting-edge AI/ML applications. Freelancing — Platforms like Upwork and Freelancer offer freelance opportunities in AI/ML.

section: Alumni Association Overview and Objectives
text:
MSAJCE Alumni Association — Overview

Alumni association of Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering welcomes you all to join us. This association is formed to reconnect and foster intellectual and emotional relationship between alumni and parent organisation. Also to bring in goodwill and voluntary commitment to support future engineers of the institution and provide industry-institute relationship and enthusiastically participate in various activities organised by the college.

Our Objectives:
1. To foster a sense of connectedness back to the institution
2. To establish and renew student relationship
3. To involve in various activities of the college
4. To provide assistance to needful students by way of funds and scholarship
5. To gather and maintain database of employment and assist in placements
6. To arrange teaching and training classes to students
7. To contribute for the overall upgrading of the institution

Alumni Registration Form: https://forms.gle/CBMdXCDEb9uXVEBv6

section: Sports Club — Introduction, Objectives, Vision, Motto and Games
text:
MSAJCE Sports Club

Introduction:
This club maintains records of sports and games events attended by students within the college, within the university and outside at the region/state/national level and their achievements/awards. It submits annual reports on sports/events and budget allocations and spending during the year. It conducts sports events that students need and rewards them with trophies and medals. All kinds of sports events take place in this club.

Objectives:
1. To build up sportsmanship.
2. To foster intimate friendships.
3. To reduce stress and improve health.
4. To improve skills and enhance employability.
5. To support in developing positive attitude, self-confidence, courage and patience.
6. To get opportunities to see the community closely and thus get an experience of human nature in relation to his/her environment.
7. To avail the opportunity to meet people from different walks of life.

Vision:
To encourage and motivate students to involve in various sports activities at both inter and intra college level.

Motto: "Teamwork Makes The Dream Work"

Games Conducted:

Outdoor Games:
Cricket, Football, Volleyball, Basketball, Rugby, Badminton, Kho Kho, Throughball

Indoor Games:
Carrom, Table Tennis, Chess

section: Fine Arts Club (ENVISTA CLUB — ARTFUL AESTHETICS) — Introduction, Objectives, Vision, Motto and Student Leaders
text:
MSAJCE Fine Arts Club — ARTFUL AESTHETICS (Part of ENVISTA CLUB)

Introduction:
Mohamed Sathak AJ College of Engineering has successfully inaugurated the ENVISTA CLUB. In total, 10 clubs are going to take part actively in this umbrella.

Objectives:
1. The main objective of the fine arts club is to encourage the budding talent of the students.
2. Impart skill-based training and multi-disciplinary research activities with industry and reputed institute collaborations. Provide opportunities and encouragement to express oneself freely.
3. Educational objectives of this club are to encourage imagination, creative ability and introduce an artistic environment.
4. Inspiring students to make maximum contributions as creative artists and citizens.
5. Organize various competitions, functions, and events in the college.
6. Make the student confident and competitive in the global environment.
7. To improve the imagination skill in entertainment.
8. To give students an artistic outlet and to help each enrich interest for art and personal experience through art.

Staff Coordinator: S. Viswajaa — Assistant Professor, IT Department

Motto:
The motto of the Fine Arts Club is to inspire and enable students to actively participate and develop their talent and skills like Dance, Art, Singing, Music, Acting etc. Through this club students can develop their skills and talents as well as feel relaxation and enjoy their college life. The club plans to conduct several events for extra-curricular activities. Students are free to participate in this club and expose their talents. The club also makes students participate in external college fests to showcase their talents.

Vision:
To encourage students to express their thoughts, feelings and creativity through various visual art forms from two dimensions to three dimensions. To tap the inherent talents and potentials of the student community at all levels.

Student President: Kishore P — IV Year Mechanical
Student Vice President: Laksha — III Year CSE

page_title: Clubs and Societies — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Science Club — Introduction, Objectives and Activities
text:
MSAJCE Science Club

Introduction:
The Science Club was started with an aim to invite eminent scientists who would share their experience with students and motivate them to gain scientific knowledge. Activities are designed primarily to introduce students to undertake interesting and exciting science and technology projects. The Science Club consists of a group of students who want to explore the many branches of engineering, mathematics, and science. The club is open to all students.

The purpose of the Science Club is to promote interest, understanding, and knowledge of the scientific world amongst college students and the local community. Science Club is one of the largest clubs in the college. The club is divided into three sections: Physics, Chemistry, and General.

The club aims to arouse engineering students' interest in learning science, promoting academic achievement in Science subjects. It also helps students to learn more about science outside the classroom and teaches how to apply scientific knowledge in daily life through activities. The club also guides students to develop an independent, logical and creative mind.

Activities include experiments in: making electronic components, making invisible ink, electroplating, biodiesels, fire writing, and making venation of leaves. Information about the science club and day-to-day updates are displayed on the notice board.

Objectives of Science Club:
The five Guiding Principles govern how activities have been developed. Investigator activity offers students the opportunity to:
1. Solve a relevant, science-based problem set within a scenario.
2. Work in pairs or small groups or independently.
3. Take part in practical, hands-on science activities.
4. Think and talk about science during the activity and when sharing results.
5. Share results using a variety of media.

Things to Do in Science Club:
- Conduct Seminars, Workshops, Exhibitions and Quiz competitions to inculcate knowledge in the field of science.
- Make students acquire knowledge by giving lectures once a month.
- Promote the use of IT in learning science.
- Encourage student participation in activities organized by the Science Club.
- Help students promote discussion on recent innovations in the field of science once a month.
- Decorate the walls of the classroom, library and laboratory with scientific pictures and charts prepared by students themselves.

page_title: Clubs and Societies — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Tamil Mandram — Introduction, Objectives and Events
text:
MSAJCE Tamil Mandram (Tamil Language and Culture Club)

Introduction:
The Tamil Mandram was formed to bring about a wonderful transformation in the youth to keep the Tamil language alive and thriving. The motto of the Mandram is:
"Thithikkum then tamil mozhi endrum vaazha vendum! Engum vella vendum!"
(Translation: "The sweet honey Tamil language must live forever! Must win everywhere!")

The Mandram works to revive and reconstruct the ancient Tamil culture and the identities left behind by ancestors. The vision is for the youth to take pride in their language and move from just speaking Tamil to actively bringing glory to Tamil through action.

"Vaazha Tamil! Velga Tamil!"
(Translation: "Long live Tamil! Victory to Tamil!")

Objectives of Tamil Mandram:
1. To develop Tamil enthusiasm and knowledge among students — this is the primary objective of the Tamil Mandram.
2. To spread ancient Tamil culture and heritage among today's youth.
3. To develop technical knowledge in Tamil.
4. To continuously conduct Tamil events and social activities suited to the times.

Events Conducted by Tamil Mandram:
1. Irumugam Oru Agam (Two faces, one heart — elocution/drama)
2. Pessum Padam (Speaking Picture — storytelling)
3. Aadu Puli (Tiger and Goat — traditional game)
4. Kaivanna Kaviyam (Colourful Poetry — art and poetry)
5. Tent Kottai (Tent Camp — cultural event)
6. Vidugadhai Vilaiyattu (Riddle Games)
7. Pirasangatin Thalaiwan (King of Speeches — debate/oratory competition)
8. Tamilkkorvai (Tamil Chain — literary event)

institution: MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering
page_title: Clubs and Societies — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Coding Club — About, Aim and Practice
text:
MSAJCE Coding Club

About:
Programming abilities are crucial tools that can be applied to and integrated into a variety of disciplines and domains in today's climate of rapid change.

Aim:
1. More coding contests should be held to reach all students on campus.
2. Conduct workshops on computer science topics like web development, application development, machine learning, IoT, etc.
3. Participate in open source initiatives, create websites and applications, and offer technical assistance to clubs and groups on campus.
4. Represent the college at intercollegiate competitions.
5. Take part in competitions like ACM-ICPC, Google Code Jam, Google Summer of Code, and other online coding events.

Practice:
1. The club holds a college-wide coding competition to find new members who are enthusiastic about coding. The competition emphasizes reasoning, analysis, and problem-solving abilities in addition to coding.
2. The club gets together once a week to talk and work on initiatives. To accomplish a shared goal, all members work together. Workshops and technical discussions on various competitions and computer science topics are conducted through the club.
3. Lecture sessions are also organized for the benefit of members, where some computer science principles are taught.
4. The group also hosts seminars and workshops by seniors and former members on how to prepare for internships and job placement.

institution: MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering

section: Robotic Club — Introduction, Aim, Objectives and Activities
text:
MSAJCE Robotic Club

Introduction:
Students that are interested in learning about and using robots can come together to form a robotics club. Teams of students must design and construct a robot that can compete against opponents in a number of distinct challenges in order to win the robotics tournament.

Aim:
The Robotics Club aims to cultivate the habit of 'thinking technologically' inside each of us.

Objectives:
1. To encourage young people to become the robotics industry's driving force.
2. It is an opportunity to creatively use information and observe how systems interact with the environment and one another.
3. To give students the professional engineering skills that will benefit them in the workplace.

Club Activities Include:
1. Robotics Education Initiatives
2. Brief Training Sessions
3. Robotics Basics Workshop

institution: MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering
page_title: Clubs and Societies — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Energy and Eco Club — Energy Club and Eco Club
text:
MSAJCE Energy and Eco Club

Energy Club:
The goal of the Energy Club is to raise and spread knowledge about responsible and sustainable energy use. Given the limited quantity of fossil fuels available, resource management and conservation are urgently needed. Students are introduced to the concept of energy conservation through the Energy Club, as well as the necessity for it and what part they can play in guaranteeing conservation. In order to combat the ongoing energy issue, it helps the students to conserve energy. These actions would support not only the dissemination of information about energy efficiency but also its application in homes and institutions.

Eco Club:
The Eco Club holds that nature imparts life lessons in its own steady ways and empowers our young students to share their responsibility for upholding environmental health and taking action to prevent environmental degradation both individually and collectively. The club encourages students to take both individual and collective action to protect the environment.

institution: MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering
page_title: Clubs and Societies — Complete Data Extract for RAG Model
section: Photography Club — Introduction and Activities
text:
MSAJCE Photography Club

Introduction:
The members of the photography club are encouraged to take images as they grow to appreciate the hobby more. It gives workers and students a venue for exploring and appreciating their surroundings through photography, short filmmaking, and screenwriting.

There is much more to photography than just using a camera to take photographs. By comprehending and learning about what constitutes a successful photograph — from composition to creative expression — students in this club have the chance to develop their skills. Additionally, club members will learn how to edit and improve photographs using cutting-edge software for both personal use and publication.

While taking pictures of sporting events and activities relevant to the school, students are encouraged to hone their talents. Exploring this fascinating aspect of the visual arts is welcomed and encouraged for people of all expertise levels.

Key Skills Developed:
1. Photography composition and creative expression
2. Photo editing using advanced software
3. Short filmmaking and screenwriting
4. Event photography (sports events, college activities)
5. Visual arts exploration

The club is open to all expertise levels — beginners and advanced photographers are both welcome.
